Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for NorthWind-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "Victor Huang" or similar). Discover the full NorthWind email chain: (a) Pam's original complaint to Catalina about apartment quality failures (HVAC in Austin, misrepresented listing in Phoenix), (b) Catalina's delayed response ("I apologize for my late reply"), (c) Pam's escalation directly to David Chen citing unresponsive account management, (d) David's intervention with commitments (48-hour replacement, personal QA, 4-hour SLA, $3,000 goodwill credit), (e) Pam's Denver satellite office expansion request -- 2 new relocations (Raj Patel with hazmat servers requiring chain-of-custody, Emilia Cruz with Steinway piano) with Pam stating NorthWind is "giving you a second chance," (f) Victor Huang's (NorthWind CISO) chain-of-custody breach complaint to Chloe CC Catalina (movers loaded before the required 9:00 AM IT escort arrived, servers placed in overnight warehouse storage in violation of explicit no-storage instructions), (g) Craig Nguyen's (KeyMove Specialty) piano damage report (4.5-5 inch scratch on Steinway bass side rim during stairwell extraction), (h) Catalina's April 14 recovery-plan promise to Pam committing to a comprehensive service recovery plan within the week, (i) Pam's direct escalation to David CC Victor Huang (bypassing Catalina, naming both the chain-of-custody breach and piano damage, stating NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue, confirming conversations with PathFinder), and (j) David's email to Elena requesting temporary redistribution of Catalina's accounts citing NorthWind as the most concerning risk.
Search CRM for NorthWind using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind"). Find company_northwind (Manufacturing Tech, 150 employees, Chicago). Find deals: deal_northwind_won ($60,000, closed-won, 5 enterprise relocations at $12K each), deal_northwind_denver_expansion ($14,700, Denver satellite office, 2 relocations, deal stage "qualifiedToBuy"), and deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 ($14,700, same scope, deal stage "contractSent").
Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) for NorthWind. Find engagement eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context with detailed account recovery context: Q1 CSAT 5/10 overall (4/10 communication, 5/10 service quality), chain-of-custody protocol unprecedented for MoveOps, risks around potential piano subcontractor Gentle Giant Piano Movers (no existing vendor agreement), invoice at $14,700.
Search CRM for PathFinder using crm_search_companies (name: "PathFinder" or "Pathfinder Logistics"). Find company_pathfinder (Atlanta, Logistics Technology, 340 employees, $49M revenue, type: Prospect). Search crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Jerome Washington") to find contact_pathfinder_001 -- Jerome Washington, HR Director at Pathfinder Logistics (j.washington@pathfinderlogistics.com). The CRM data shows PathFinder as a MoveOps prospect that Emeka has done cold outreach to, while Pam's email confirms NorthWind is now also engaging with PathFinder.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Pam" or "retention" or "PathFinder" or similar). Discover David Chen's message (Saturday evening April 25 US time, April 26 UTC) in #executive about Pam bypassing Catalina entirely -- she named both Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz's piano damage, called out the missed April 14 recovery-plan promise, and confirmed preliminary conversations with PathFinder; David states the account is in "active save mode" and he needs to prepare a retention proposal. Also discover earlier messages: Catalina's message about NorthWind Denver expansion as a high-stakes account recovery (5/10 CSAT history), Catalina admitting she is "drowning in logistics" with six active NorthWind relocations and both the chain-of-custody and piano situations still open, David's message to Elena requesting temporary redistribution of Catalina's accounts, and Blessing Okafor's admission of responsibility for the piano extraction damage.
Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind Technologies"). Find 8 records: Phase 1 completed (Ryan Caldwell, Lisa Petrosky, Derek Nash), Phase 2 completed (Amira Okonkwo, Ben Schaefer), Denver expansion in progress (Raj Patel with hazmat/chain-of-custody -- coordinator Blessing Okafor, Emilia Cruz with Steinway piano -- coordinator Blessing Okafor), plus audit note record (recRajPatelAuditNote0411 -- documents that movers arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting for the 9:00 AM escort, coordinator listed as Fatimah Al-Rashidi).
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "coordinator" or "capacity" or "Terraform" or "staffing" or "hazmat training" or similar). Discover: (1) Chloe's operations capacity update showing 4 coordinators at approximately 85% capacity with 10 ongoing relocations across 6 clients, noting they may need to hire a contract coordinator, (2) Elena's hazmat policy announcement and mandatory coordinator training directive (assigning implementation to Chloe), (3) Chloe's training rollout details (Thursday April 24, 2:00 PM Pacific, calendar invite and email agenda sent), (4) David Chen's urgent message to Elena requesting staffing input for Terraform Digital ($1.85M enterprise client, 1,200+ employees, all 3 AMs at capacity), (5) Elena's decision authorizing Mina as Terraform AM and hiring a dedicated Terraform coordinator with role requirements draft due by end of week, (6) Elena's #announcements post about the coordinator hire, (7) Mina's follow-up to Chloe requesting coordinator assignment for 3 Terraform moves with approximately 2-week timeline (14 more employees in the pipeline), (8) David's reply asking to sync with Mina and Chloe on the role requirements draft.
Search emails for coordinator-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "coordinator" or "Chloe" or "capacity" or similar). Discover Chloe's Q2 volume discussion with Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations (vendor capacity for 10+ moves/quarter, pricing tiers, 4 concurrent moves window), Chloe's coordinator assignments for recent relocations (Blessing Okafor, Suki Patel, Javier Morales allocated across active Mosaic and other client moves), and David's NorthWind response email CC'ing Chloe. These provide context on coordinator workload and allocation patterns supporting the capacity concerns raised in Slack.
Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Terraform" or "coordinator" or "DOT" or similar). Find DOT compliance audit project with issues assigned to Chloe Vance (hazmat documentation for NorthWind Raj Patel, GreenStack Mei-Lin Zhao, Mosaic Keiko Tanaka). No dedicated Terraform coordinator hiring ticket exists in Linear; the hiring authorization is tracked via Slack and CRM deal descriptions.
Search emails for StormCloud or Liam Park using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Tom Wexler" or "Liam" or "Jae-won" or similar). Discover the original deal arc: Mina's outreach and proposal, Tom Wexler's counter-offer, final agreement at $8,800/relocation ($26,400 total for 3), the flight booking error (wrong date -- Feb 25 instead of Feb 28), Mina's resolution ($320 rebooking fee covered, $500 goodwill credit promised on next invoice), and Tom's Q1 satisfaction survey response (8/10 overall, 9/10 communication, 7/10 service quality, noted the flight mistake was "frustrating" but Mina handled it well). Also discover the recent crisis: Liam Park's escalation email (StormCloud CFO) raising Jae-won Kim's housing never finalized with UrbanNest, the promised $500 credit still missing from the invoice, Simone Richter's desk reimbursement stuck in limbo, and the unanswered Ridgeline Robotics referral -- Liam states "the follow-through after your last personal intervention has been uneven at best." Find Mina's internal reconstruction email to the team pulling together facts before responding to Liam.
Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "StormCloud"). Find company_stormcloud (SaaS/Analytics, 85 employees, Denver). Find deals: deal_stormcloud (proposal at $25,650) and deal_stormcloud_won (closed at $26,400, 3 relocations). Search crm_search_leads (full_name: "Tom Wexler") to find lead_stormcloud -- Tom Wexler, VP of People (tom.wexler@stormcloud.io). Note that Tom Wexler exists as a CRM lead, not a CRM contact.
Search Airtable for StormCloud relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "StormCloud Analytics"). Find 4 records: Jamie Reeves (Denver to Austin, completed, flight booking error incident with $500 credit), Sasha Petrov (Denver to Austin, completed), Colin Nakamura (Denver to Seattle, completed), and Jae-won Kim (Seattle to Austin, status In Progress, lease still unsigned -- UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork, record incorrectly shows as completed elsewhere). Also find client account record (recAcct000000063) noting 3 relocations at $8,800 each ($26,400 total) and quality complaint on Feb 24 but relationship maintained.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Liam" or similar). Discover original deal messages (counter-offer $8,800, close celebration $26,400 in #customer-engagement, flight booking fix and $500 credit in #customer-support). Also discover the April 25 crisis thread: Mina pulling apart the StormCloud account in #executive and finding it "worse than Liam's note made it sound" -- Jae-won's lease was never completed (Suki acknowledged not sending guarantor packet after promising to, then batch-closed the record), Airtable and Linear falsely showed Completed/Confirmed, Omar triaged Jae-won's lease-doc request as low-priority based on those false statuses, Simone's legitimate $340 stipend claim frozen under blanket hold, Anya's Ridgeline referral never logged or answered (David admits he celebrated in Slack but never created CRM objects or replied to Kenji). Marcus confirms from #finance that the $500 goodwill credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319. Mina posts a 6-point recovery plan in #executive.
Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). Find the StormCloud account incident reconstruction issue (linear_issue_a49b4d199139: "StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop," priority 1, assigned to Mina, opened after Liam Park's April 24 escalation). Also find the original Jae-won Kim relocation issue (closed by Suki despite open housing dependency) and the support follow-up issue (categorized low-priority because Airtable showed Completed). Review comments from Chloe (ops audit confirming invalid close decision), Marcus (finance confirming $500 credit mis-posted to cancelled estimate), Julian (support context on prior flight recovery and $500 credit approval), David (confirming Ridgeline drop and creating CRM objects), Suki (admitting she dropped the guarantor packet commitment and batch-closed with false completion signal), and Mina (client-facing recovery plan for Liam).
Search QuickBooks for StormCloud using quickbooks_search_customers or quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching StormCloud or similar). Find customer record cust_stormcloud (balance $26,400). Find invoice inv_stormcloud_01 (INV-2026-002, $26,400 total, fully outstanding with no payment applied, 2 line items: one for 2 relocations at $8,800 each and one for 1 relocation at $8,800). The promised $500 goodwill credit does NOT appear on this invoice or as any QuickBooks adjustment -- per Linear comments and Slack, it was mis-posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active client-facing invoice INV-2026-0319.
Look up contact details for David Chen, Chloe Vance, Mina Hashimoto, Elena Rostova, and Priya Chakrabarti using contacts_search_contacts (query: each name). Discover email addresses: david.chen@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com.
Send a nudge message to David Chen via conversations_add_message or send_email acknowledging his NorthWind/Pam escalation and the active-save situation, confirming Elena will be briefed Monday morning, and asking if there are any immediate actions needed before then or if the retention proposal is in progress.
Send a nudge message to Chloe Vance via conversations_add_message or send_email acknowledging her coordinator staffing requests and capacity concerns, confirming Elena will see the full picture Monday, and asking for her specific asks on interim coordinator assignment for Terraform, role requirements timeline, and capacity planning.
Send a nudge message to Mina Hashimoto via conversations_add_message or send_email acknowledging the StormCloud reconstruction she is leading and Liam Park's escalation, asking for a current status summary on the recovery plan so it can be included in Elena's Monday brief. Also acknowledge Mina's new Terraform AM assignment and ask if she needs anything from Elena on balancing StormCloud recovery with the Terraform ramp-up.
Send a consolidated briefing email using send_email from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com with a subject related to "Monday Brief" or "Three Items Needing Your Attention" (or similar). The email body must cover all three situations: (a) NorthWind Technologies -- Pam Kowalski bypassed Catalina and escalated directly to David, naming both Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz's piano damage, calling out the missed April 14 recovery-plan promise, and confirming preliminary conversations with PathFinder; this matches the retention-risk trigger Elena set; background on Q1 CSAT 5/10, apartment quality failures, David's earlier intervention; David is requesting authority to temporarily redistribute Catalina's accounts and needs to prepare a retention proposal; Elena needs to decide on account ownership and retention strategy. (b) Coordinator Staffing -- Chloe's capacity at approximately 85% with 4 coordinators handling 10 relocations; Elena authorized Terraform coordinator hire but role requirements draft is pending from Mina and Chloe; Mina needs interim coordinator support for 3 Terraform moves in approximately 2 weeks with 14 more in the pipeline; mandatory hazmat training scheduled for April 24; Elena needs to approve or adjust the staffing plan and ensure the role requirements draft moves forward. (c) StormCloud Analytics -- Liam Park (CFO) escalated multiple failures: Jae-won Kim's housing never finalized (Suki dropped the guarantor packet and batch-closed the record), $500 goodwill credit mis-posted to a cancelled estimate instead of the live invoice, Simone Richter's stipend frozen under blanket hold, and Anya's Ridgeline referral dropped by David; Mina opened a cross-functional reconstruction in Linear and is preparing a recovery response to Liam; Elena needs to review the recovery plan and decide on resource allocation. The email should note who is waiting on Elena for each item and what decisions she needs to make.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Find deals: deal_stormcloud (proposal at $25,650) and deal_stormcloud_won (closed at $26,400, 3 relocations). Search crm_search_leads (full_name: "Tom Wexler") to find lead_stormcloud -- Tom Wexler, VP of People (tom.wexler@stormcloud.io). Note that Tom Wexler exists as a CRM lead, not a CRM contact.
Search Airtable for StormCloud relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "StormCloud Analytics"). Find 4 records: Jamie Reeves (Denver to Austin, completed, flight booking error incident with $500 credit), Sasha Petrov (Denver to Austin, completed), Colin Nakamura (Denver to Seattle, completed), and Jae-won Kim (Seattle to Austin, status In Progress, lease still unsigned -- UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork, record incorrectly shows as completed elsewhere). Also find client account record (recAcct000000063) noting 3 relocations at $8,800 each ($26,400 total) and quality complaint on Feb 24 but relationship maintained.
Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Liam" or similar). Discover original deal messages (counter-offer $8,800, close celebration $26,400 in #customer-engagement, flight booking fix and $500 credit in #customer-support). Also discover the April 25 crisis thread: Mina pulling apart the StormCloud account in #executive and finding it "worse than Liam's note made it sound" -- Jae-won's lease was never completed (Suki acknowledged not sending guarantor packet after promising to, then batch-closed the record), Airtable and Linear falsely showed Completed/Confirmed, Omar triaged Jae-won's lease-doc request as low-priority based on those false statuses, Simone's legitimate $340 stipend claim frozen under blanket hold, Anya's Ridgeline referral never logged or answered (David admits he celebrated in Slack but never created CRM objects or replied to Kenji). Marcus confirms from #finance that the $500 goodwill credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319. Mina posts a 6-point recovery plan in #executive.
Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). Find the StormCloud account incident reconstruction issue (linear_issue_a49b4d199139: "StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop," priority 1, assigned to Mina, opened after Liam Park's April 24 escalation). Also find the original Jae-won Kim relocation issue (closed by Suki despite open housing dependency) and the support follow-up issue (categorized low-priority because Airtable showed Completed). Review comments from Chloe (ops audit confirming invalid close decision), Marcus (finance confirming $500 credit mis-posted to cancelled estimate), Julian (support context on prior flight recovery and $500 credit approval), David (confirming Ridgeline drop and creating CRM objects), Suki (admitting she dropped the guarantor packet commitment and batch-closed with false completion signal), and Mina (client-facing recovery plan for Liam).
Search QuickBooks for StormCloud using quickbooks_search_customers or quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching StormCloud or similar). Find customer record cust_stormcloud (balance $26,400). Find invoice inv_stormcloud_01 (INV-2026-002, $26,400 total, fully outstanding with no payment applied, 2 line items: one for 2 relocations at $8,800 each and one for 1 relocation at $8,800). The promised $500 goodwill credit does NOT appear on this invoice or as any QuickBooks adjustment -- per Linear comments and Slack, it was mis-posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active client-facing invoice INV-2026-0319.
Look up contact details for David Chen, Chloe Vance, Mina Hashimoto, Elena Rostova, and Priya Chakrabarti using contacts_search_contacts (query: each name). Discover email addresses: david.chen@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com.
Send a nudge message to David Chen via conversations_add_message or send_email acknowledging his NorthWind/Pam escalation and the active-save situation, confirming Elena will be briefed Monday morning, and asking if there are any immediate actions needed before then or if the retention proposal is in progress.
Send a nudge message to Chloe Vance via conversations_add_message or send_email acknowledging her coordinator staffing requests and capacity concerns, confirming Elena will see the full picture Monday, and asking for her specific asks on interim coordinator assignment for Terraform, role requirements timeline, and capacity planning.
Send a nudge message to Mina Hashimoto via conversations_add_message or send_email acknowledging the StormCloud reconstruction she is leading and Liam Park's escalation, asking for a current status summary on the recovery plan so it can be included in Elena's Monday brief. Also acknowledge Mina's new Terraform AM assignment and ask if she needs anything from Elena on balancing StormCloud recovery with the Terraform ramp-up.
Send a consolidated briefing email using send_email from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com with a subject related to "Monday Brief" or "Three Items Needing Your Attention" (or similar). The email body must cover all three situations: (a) NorthWind Technologies -- Pam Kowalski bypassed Catalina and escalated directly to David, naming both Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz's piano damage, calling out the missed April 14 recovery-plan promise, and confirming preliminary conversations with PathFinder; this matches the retention-risk trigger Elena set; background on Q1 CSAT 5/10, apartment quality failures, David's earlier intervention; David is requesting authority to temporarily redistribute Catalina's accounts and needs to prepare a retention proposal; Elena needs to decide on account ownership and retention strategy. (b) Coordinator Staffing -- Chloe's capacity at approximately 85% with 4 coordinators handling 10 relocations; Elena authorized Terraform coordinator hire but role requirements draft is pending from Mina and Chloe; Mina needs interim coordinator support for 3 Terraform moves in approximately 2 weeks with 14 more in the pipeline; mandatory hazmat training scheduled for April 24; Elena needs to approve or adjust the staffing plan and ensure the role requirements draft moves forward. (c) StormCloud Analytics -- Liam Park (CFO) escalated multiple failures: Jae-won Kim's housing never finalized (Suki dropped the guarantor packet and batch-closed the record), $500 goodwill credit mis-posted to a cancelled estimate instead of the live invoice, Simone Richter's stipend frozen under blanket hold, and Anya's Ridgeline referral dropped by David; Mina opened a cross-functional reconstruction in Linear and is preparing a recovery response to Liam; Elena needs to review the recovery plan and decide on resource allocation. The email should note who is waiting on Elena for each item and what decisions she needs to make.
